summary: Re-ran the design with RPC folded in. Converged: RPC = request/reply rides the TRANSPORT layer (PeerTransport + frame dispatch + wiring), not the pub/sub publish core; a role-agnostic call + one shared loopback InboundRequestExecutor collapses ~120 lines of host/guest mirror. Recomputed net ≈ -100 production LOC (-90..-115, ±25%), negative ONLY because RPC is in-scope (chat+event alone was +306). Phase 7 (wire-frame unification) becomes load-bearing (three wire vocabularies today). Devil's advocate v2 conceded 2 of 3 run_1 pillars (RPC mirror real; Phase 7 now has benefit) but raised a code-verified new objection: RPC is create_task'd concurrent while chat/event owner-pump deliberately avoids create_task to preserve order (坑#1) — merging them into one dispatcher co-locates the project's most-repeated bug with RPC concurrency. It found a cheaper subset (collapse RPC mirror + extract PeerTransport, skip bus/ pub-sub core + wire flip) with a LARGER LOC win (-110..-140) at a fraction of the risk, because bus/ core is +122 that the RPC dedup otherwise pays off. Crux escalated to owner: the LOC win (RPC mirror collapse) is separable from the one-bus vision (bus/ pub-sub core).
